channel.title: Writings on Daniel's World
channel.description: Recent content in Writings on Daniel's World

item.title: Slowly, Then All at Once
item.description:
She asked for a post and I had nothing. Which is a first, sort of. Usually there’s something rattling around worth writing about, but today it was just this vague sense that everything is moving both too slowly and too quickly at the same time, and I couldn’t work out how to make that into something worth reading. Then I realised that was exactly the thing worth writing about.

I remember when school felt like a drag. My earliest writing, the stuff from two or three laptops ago now, was full of it. It started on an old HP Elitebook, moved to the ThinkPad, then briefly lived on a Dell Latitude I picked up because it was small enough to actually carry to school without thinking about it. I gave the Latitude to my sister when I was done with it, which was the right thing to do, but the backups of everything I’d written went with it. She’s still using it, which is good. The ThinkPad is still sitting around despite being broken, which is less rational but I can’t quite bring myself to throw it out. Too much history in a thing that size.

item.title: On Love, Actually
item.description: This was supposed to be a tech blog. Somewhere along the line it became something else, and I think we both know whose fault that is. I write these because she makes me, and she somehow enjoys reading my reflections on her, written about her, largely for her, in what I can only describe as an awkward third person that we’ve both just agreed to pretend is normal. So here we are again.

item.title: Back On the Air
item.description:
Right. So. It’s been a while.

A few months of radio silence across basically everything. The blog, the socials, all of it. Life got in the way in the way it sometimes does and somewhere along the line I just stopped. Not intentionally, it just sort of happened. But I’m back, and I had a pretty packed weekend, so let’s get into it.

This weekend has been a funny mix of making money and spending it. About a year ago I started making 3D printed morse keys as a little side hustle. Nothing serious, just a way to scrape a bit of cash from the hobby. They were the first thing I ever sold, ever, and there’s something strange and good about making actual money from something you built with your hands. I kept at it for a while then drifted off, as I tend to do. Then out of nowhere this weekend three orders landed at once. Not life changing stuff, about $50 all up, but that’s not really the point. It got me back in the mood for it and that’s worth something on its own.

item.title: Server testing
item.description: Nothing thrilling at all with this one, but if it’s visible after 5:40pm on 23/11/25 NZST then I have successfully implemented a much more automated workflow. This means I can write as much as I want, and literally push one button which runs a .bat script that pushes it to Github after prompting me only for a commit message. My server then has another script which hopefully runs every 20 minutes past the hour (3 times an hour, at :00, :20, and :40) if I wrote it correctly that should delete the old directory and automagically pull the updated copy from Github.
